preamble: The diphthongization of a mid-front nasalized vowel [ẽ] to [ei] is a common historical development, particularly in some Romance languages where nasalized vowels are often produced with a following glide.

examples.note: Development where the nasal vowel phoneme [ẽ] is produced as a diphthong [vẽj̃], mapped here to [ẽ] > [ei].

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
